scope: Survey of three accessibility categories that are not explicitly handled by Owlory today: (1) Reduce Motion / Reduce Transparency / Increase Contrast / Differentiate Without Color, (2) Haptic + sensory feedback, (3) Switch Control / Voice Control input labels / accessibility rotors and custom actions. Audit-only; no code changes.
method: grep `.animation\(|withAnimation|\.transition\(` across owlory_xcode/Owlory/**/*.swift for motion surfaces.
grep `OwloryColor\.(success|warning|error|...)` plus inspection of nearby icons/text for color-only state indicators.
grep `UIImpactFeedback|UINotificationFeedback|UISelectionFeedback|sensoryFeedback|SensoryFeedback` to confirm haptic absence (zero matches).
grep `accessibilityCustomActions|accessibilityRotor|accessibilityAction|accessibilityInputLabels` to confirm rotor + custom-action absence (zero matches).
grep `\.swipeActions` to inventory swipe-gated actions that need a Switch Control / VoiceOver equivalent.
grep `@Environment\(\\.accessibility(ReduceMotion|ReduceTransparency|DifferentiateWithoutColor|...)` to confirm absence of adaptive environment reads (zero matches).

classification_legend.none: No code path observes or honors the accessibility category at any of the relevant surfaces.
classification_legend.partial: Some surfaces honor the category but coverage is incomplete or inconsistent.
classification_legend.present: Surface explicitly observes the accessibility category; documented behavior matches Apple HIG.
classification_legend.n/a: Category does not apply to this surface.

categories.name: Reduce Motion / Reduce Transparency / Increase Contrast / Differentiate Without Color
categories.category_summary: No `@Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion)`, `\.accessibilityReduceTransparency`, `\.colorSchemeContrast`, or `\.accessibilityDifferentiateWithoutColor` reads anywhere in the app. Animations and transparency are applied unconditionally. Some state indicators pair color with an icon (mitigating the differentiate-without-color concern), but several rely on color alone.

findings.surface: DesignSystem/AppTheme.swift:94 — withAnimation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.2))
findings.current_behavior: Animation runs regardless of the user's Reduce Motion preference.
findings.recommended: Read @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion); when true, skip the withAnimation block (apply the state change without animation).

findings.surface: Features/Today/TodayView.swift:949 — `.transition(.opacity)` on reflection-saved confirmation
findings.current_behavior: Opacity transition fades in/out unconditionally.
findings.recommended: Gate the `.transition` modifier on `!reduceMotion`. Fall back to `.identity`.

findings.surface: Features/Today/TodayView.swift:1195 — `.animation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.15), value: value)` on metric chip
findings.current_behavior: Implicit animation fires on every value change.
findings.recommended: Skip the .animation modifier when reduceMotion is true.

findings.surface: Features/Train/TrainView.swift:536 — same .animation pattern on metric chip
findings.current_behavior: Same shape as M03.
findings.recommended: Same fix as M03.

findings.surface: Features/Today/TodayView.swift:953, 1377 — withAnimation { ... }
findings.current_behavior: Two more unguarded withAnimation blocks.
findings.recommended: Wrap with reduceMotion guard or extract a helper `animatedIfAllowed(action:)`.

findings.surface: Multiple .opacity(0.08-0.30) calls across DesignSystem + TodayView + TrainView (~10 sites)
findings.current_behavior: Color overlays use semitransparent fills unconditionally (e.g., `OwloryColor.brandPrimary.opacity(0.10)`, `OwloryColor.warning.opacity(0.12)`).
findings.recommended: Read @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceTransparency); when true, fall back to a solid fill at higher contrast (e.g., the same hue at full alpha or a paired token like `brandPrimaryHigh`). Lowest-impact fix is a single tokenized helper that returns either the alpha-blended color or a solid alternate.

findings.surface: Features/Home/HomeView.swift:1233-1236 — Image(systemName: 'checkmark.circle.fill') + 'forward.circle.fill'
findings.current_behavior: State is conveyed by SF Symbol shape AND OwloryColor.success/warning. Color and shape are both present — this is OK by HIG.
findings.recommended: No change needed; confirm during a visual audit that all status indicators pair shape + color.

findings.surface: Features/Today/TodayView.swift:1243 — `case 1...2: return OwloryColor.error` (severity tint)
findings.current_behavior: Returns a color based on a numeric severity score. Need to trace to see if the consuming UI also varies shape/weight/border, or relies on color alone.
findings.recommended: Inspect the caller. If color is the only signal, add a shape/icon/label cue (e.g., severity icon, text annotation).

findings.surface: Features/Train/TrainView.swift:380-385 — status pill background + border at varying opacities
findings.current_behavior: Pill background is `statusPillColor(s).opacity(0.15)` and border `.opacity(0.3)`. Selection vs unselected is conveyed only by these alpha differences.
findings.recommended: Under reduceTransparency, use solid background + stronger border. Under increaseContrast (@Environment colorSchemeContrast == .increased), bump opacity and/or border weight.

findings.surface: All Feature views — no @Environment reads for any of the four accessibility settings
findings.category: All four (Reduce Motion / Reduce Transparency / Increase Contrast / Differentiate Without Color)
findings.current_behavior: Zero awareness of the four user settings in the codebase.
findings.recommended: Introduce a thin AccessibilitySettings observable (or per-view @Environment reads) that exposes the four flags. Adoption then becomes a per-view modifier choice rather than an audit of every animation/opacity call.

categories.name: Haptic / sensory feedback
categories.category_summary: Zero usages of UIImpactFeedback / UINotificationFeedback / UISelectionFeedback / SwiftUI .sensoryFeedback anywhere in the app. Several user actions that would conventionally provide tactile confirmation provide none.

findings.surface: Features/Home/HomeView.swift — task completion (swipe action 'Mark complete' + Today's 'Complete' button)
findings.current_behavior: Marks task complete with visual feedback only.
findings.recommended: .sensoryFeedback(.success, trigger: completedAt) — light success notification when a task transitions to completed.

findings.surface: Features/Today/TodayView.swift — Add to Focus action
findings.current_behavior: Adds a row to Focus Three with visual confirmation; no haptic.
findings.recommended: .sensoryFeedback(.selection, trigger: focusCount) — selection-style click on adding an item.

findings.surface: DesignSystem/VoiceCaptureButton.swift — start/stop recording
findings.current_behavior: Toggles recording state with visual feedback only.
findings.recommended: .sensoryFeedback(.impact(weight: .medium), trigger: isRecording) — medium impact at start and stop so the user knows the recording boundary.

findings.surface: DesignSystem/AudioPlaybackButton.swift — playback start/stop
findings.current_behavior: Same shape as H03.
findings.recommended: .sensoryFeedback(.selection, trigger: isPlaying).

findings.surface: Five .alert presentations across Train/Write/Home/Career/Today views (the 'Couldn't Update X' family)
findings.current_behavior: Alert appears with visual feedback only.
findings.recommended: .sensoryFeedback(.error, trigger: hasError) — error notification when an alert is presented.

findings.surface: Features/Home/HomeView.swift — protocol step completion (skip/complete swipe actions)
findings.current_behavior: Same shape as H01.
findings.recommended: .sensoryFeedback(.success, trigger: stepCompletedAt) for complete; .sensoryFeedback(.selection, trigger: stepSkippedAt) for skip.

categories.name: Switch Control / Voice Control input labels / accessibility rotors and custom actions
categories.category_summary: Zero accessibilityCustomActions, accessibilityRotor, or accessibilityInputLabels usages. The app has 12 swipeActions call sites whose actions are reachable only via swipe gestures — Switch Control and VoiceOver users have no equivalent way to invoke them unless the same action is also exposed as a tappable button.

findings.surface: Features/Write/WriteView.swift:176, 186, 240, 248 — 4 swipeActions on note rows (advance to source, archive, restore, etc.)
findings.current_behavior: Actions are reachable only via swipe; no `.accessibilityAction(named:)` or `.accessibilityAction(.default)` provides a VoiceOver / Switch Control equivalent.
findings.recommended: Add `.accessibilityAction(named: L("write.row.advance")) { ... }` for each swipe action so VoiceOver users can invoke them via the rotor and Switch Control users can reach them via tab traversal.

findings.surface: Features/Home/HomeView.swift:230, 274, 347, 563, 1184 — 5 swipeActions on task / run rows
findings.current_behavior: Same shape as S01. The existing localized hint `home.task.accessibility.advanceHint` etc. tells the VoiceOver user the swipe gesture exists, but the gesture itself isn't VoiceOver-invokable without `accessibilityAction`.
findings.recommended: Same fix as S01. Existing `home.task.accessibility.markComplete` and family already exist as localized labels — these become the `named:` argument.

findings.surface: Features/Today/TodayView.swift — 2 swipeActions on Continue rows (continuePrimarySwipeActions + continueStatusSwipeActions)
findings.current_behavior: Same shape as S01-S02.
findings.recommended: Same fix; localized strings `today.continue.accessibility.focusStatusActions` and `today.continue.accessibility.addToFocus` already exist as hints — promote them to `named:` action labels.

findings.surface: Features/Career/CareerView.swift:136 — 1 swipeActions
findings.current_behavior: Same shape.
findings.recommended: Same fix.

findings.surface: Features/Home/HomeView.swift + Features/Today/TodayView.swift — dense lists of tasks / Continue rows
findings.current_behavior: VoiceOver users navigate row-by-row. No rotor entry helps them jump to category boundaries (e.g., today vs upcoming, focused vs all).
findings.recommended: Add `.accessibilityRotor("Focused tasks")` and similar rotors on the Today and Home views, populated by the IDs of focused / category-leading rows. Low-priority — only worth doing once the rows are dense enough to feel painful.

findings.surface: Voice Control across all custom-styled controls
findings.current_behavior: Voice Control falls back to the `accessibilityLabel` as the spoken command. For most controls the label is a sentence (e.g., 'Mark Wash dishes complete'). Voice Control users would have to say the full sentence.
findings.recommended: For high-frequency controls (mark complete, skip, edit, add to focus, start recording), add `.accessibilityInputLabels([...])` with short verb-noun pairs alongside the existing accessibilityLabel. Example: `["Complete", "Mark complete", "Done"]`.

findings.surface: DesignSystem/VoiceCaptureButton.swift + AudioPlaybackButton.swift
findings.current_behavior: Both buttons use accessibilityLabel only. No accessibilityCustomActions for secondary behaviors (e.g., pause, scrub).
findings.recommended: If pause / scrub are eventually added, expose them via accessibilityCustomActions to avoid hiding them behind gestures.

switch_voice_rotors_actions.highest_severity: swipeActions invisible to Switch Control + VoiceOver users (12 sites)

follow_up_slices_recommended.scope: Add `.accessibilityAction(named:)` for each swipe action across Write/Home/Today/Career views (S01-S04, 12 sites). Use the existing localized hint strings (write.row.accessibility.*, home.task.accessibility.*, today.continue.accessibility.*) as the action names. No new strings needed in the happy path.

Introduce `@Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion)` reads in TodayView + TrainView + AppTheme; gate the 5 animation/transition sites (M01-M05) on the flag. Add a small `withAnimationIfAllowed(_ animation:_:)` helper if the pattern repeats.

Add `.sensoryFeedback` to the 6 user actions named in H01-H06 (task completion, focus add, recording start/stop, playback toggle, error alert presentation, protocol step). No new strings; per-call only.
Address M06, M09 (and the M08 trace) by introducing tokenized fills that adapt to reduceTransparency + colorSchemeContrast. Touches OwloryColor + DesignSystem + the immediate Train/Today consumers.

Add `.accessibilityInputLabels` for high-frequency Voice Control commands (mark complete, skip, edit, add to focus, start recording). Localizable strings get a new namespace `*.voicecontrol.label.*` so non-English speakers get locale-appropriate short forms.

explicit_non_audit_findings: VoiceOver coverage is already strong (20 labels + 5 hints + 1 value + custom elements). This audit does not duplicate that; it focuses on the other categories.
Dynamic Type is also already supported (12 dynamicTypeSize references + SwiftUI's automatic scaling via .font modifiers). Not in scope here.

Some color-only states are already paired with shape (M07: checkmark.circle.fill + success color), so Differentiate Without Color is partially handled even without an environment read. The remaining concern is the Train status pill (M09) and Today severity tint (M08).
not_claimed: Every animation, transparency, or color usage has been inventoried. The findings name the highest-traffic surfaces; smaller decorative animations may exist.

Every swipeActions site has been traced to confirm whether the same action is also reachable via a button. Two sites might already have a tappable equivalent in their detail view, in which case the accessibilityAction is redundant; the follow-up slice should verify per-site.
Switch Control physical-device testing has been done. The findings are static-scan and HIG-pattern-based; on-device confirmation of focus order and dwell timing remains.
Voice Control device testing has been done. The recommendation for accessibilityInputLabels is based on labels-are-long; device confirmation that voice commands actually fail on long labels remains.
Reduce Motion, Reduce Transparency, or Increase Contrast have been device-tested under the actual settings. Findings reflect code shape, not on-device behavior.
